item.title: Factors associated with urgent consultation recommendations for patients with inflammatory bowel disease calling a telephone service about worsening symptoms: a secondary analysis.
Defining Moderate Disease Activity and Severity in Crohn's Disease and Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Journal of clinical gastroenterology - This research aimed to review the literature for definitions of moderate Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC). Real-world evidence suggests that biologics may achieve better outcomes in...

item.title: [Use of NSAIDs in inflammatory bowel disease].
item.description: Lakartidningen - Systematic review - Health Technology Assessment (HTA) Region Stockholm conducted a systematic review evaluating the risk of flare-ups from use of non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAID) in patients with inflamm...

item.title: Enhancing Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) competency in gastroenterology fellowship through traditional, digital, and experiential learning pathways.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The rapidly evolving field of Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) places growing educational demands on gastroenterology trainees, many of whom report gaps in confidence and preparedness in managing I...

item.title: [Guideline for the Treatment of Ulcerative Colitis and Crohn's Disease in Adult Patients].
item.description: Revista medica de Chile - Systematic review - Ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease are chronic conditions that can take a potentially severe or even life-threatening course, with high morbidity stemming from their complications. These diseas...
